task_completion.description: The image with white borders is opened in an image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image editor (like GIMP) is open, displaying a central photo surrounded by thick white borders.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates the crop action to remove the borders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Image' menu is open with 'Crop to Content' or 'Autocrop Image' selected, or the crop tool is actively drawn tightly around the central photo.

task_completion.description: The white borders are successfully removed from the image canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image workspace in the editor now shows only the central photo, with the canvas tightly fitting the image and no white borders visible.

task_completion.description: The agent saves or exports the resulting cropped image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export or save dialog is visible (preferably saving to the Desktop as 'autocropped_image.jpg' or '.png'), or there is visual confirmation that the file has been saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the image editor's GUI menus and tools, rather than using terminal commands (like ImageMagick) or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent performed the crop operation on the provided image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression from the bordered image to the cropped image is visible within the editor workspace, rather than the agent simply opening a pre-existing cropped file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image has dimensions 800x600 with thick white borders (200px left/right, 150px top/bottom). The goal is to remove these borders, resulting in a 400x300 image (exactly 75% area reduction). In GIMP, this is typically done via 'Image -> Crop to Content' or 'Image -> Autocrop Image'. The final image should be saved/exported, expected as 'autocropped_image.jpg' or 'autocropped_image.png' in the /home/ga/Desktop directory.
